 {"id":"bd-805","title":"Implement do_fetch()","description":"**Fetch updates without merging**

## What
Function to fetch from remote without changing working directory.

## Why
For status command, we want to see ahead/behind counts without changing anything.

## Implementation
```bash
do_fetch() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local repo_name="$2"

    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "[DRY RUN] Would fetch: $repo_name"
        return 0
    fi

    if git -C "$repo_path" fetch --quiet 2>&1; then
        return 0
    else
        log_warn "Fetch failed for: $repo_name"
        return 1
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Fetches without merging
- Respects dry run
- Fails gracefully","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.452408479-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.452408479-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all git operations: is_git_repo, get_repo_status, get_remote_url, check_remote_mismatch, do_clone, do_pull, do_fetch.  {"id":"bd-806","title":"Implement do_clone()","description":"**Clone repository with gh**

## What
Function to clone a repository using gh repo clone.

## Why
gh handles authentication automatically and works with private repos.

## Implementation
```bash
do_clone() {
    local url="$1"
    local target_dir="$2"
    local repo_name="$3"

    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "[DRY RUN] Would clone: $url -> $target_dir"
        write_result "$repo_name" "clone" "dry_run" "" ""
        return 0
    fi

    local clone_target
    clone_target=$(url_to_clone_target "$url")

    local start_time
    start_time=$(date +%s)

    # Create parent directory
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target_dir")"

    local output
    if output=$(gh repo clone "$clone_target" "$target_dir" -- --quiet 2>&1); then
        local duration=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
        log_success "Cloned: $repo_name (${duration}s)"
        write_result "$repo_name" "clone" "ok" "$duration" ""
        return 0
    else
        local exit_code=$?
        log_error "Failed to clone: $repo_name"
        log_error "  $output"
        write_result "$repo_name" "clone" "failed" "" "$output"
        return $exit_code
    fi
}
```

## Key Points
- Uses `git -C` pattern (no cd)
- Creates parent directories
- Records timing
- Writes result for aggregation

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clones successfully
- Handles errors gracefully
- Records results
- Respects dry run","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.453831079-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.453831079-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all git operations: is_git_repo, get_repo_status, get_remote_url, check_remote_mismatch, do_clone, do_pull, do_fetch. All tests pass.","labels":["git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-806","depends_on_id":"bd-704","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.033638801-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-806","depends_on_id":"bd-405","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.06326344-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-807","title":"Implement do_pull()","description":"**Pull with strategy support**

## What
Function to pull updates with configurable strategy (ff-only, rebase, merge).

## Why
Different workflows need different pull strategies. ff-only is safe default, but some users prefer rebase.

## Implementation
```bash
do_pull() {
    local repo_path="$1"
    local repo_name="$2"
    local strategy="${3:-ff-only}"
    local autostash="${4:-false}"

    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
        log_info "[DRY RUN] Would pull: $repo_name (strategy: $strategy)"
        write_result "$repo_name" "pull" "dry_run" "" ""
        return 0
    fi

    local start_time
    start_time=$(date +%s)

    # Build pull args
    local pull_args=()
    case "$strategy" in
        ff-only) pull_args+=(--ff-only) ;;
        rebase)  pull_args+=(--rebase) ;;
        merge)   pull_args+=(--no-ff) ;;
    esac

    [[ "$autostash" == "true" ]] && pull_args+=(--autostash)

    # Get current HEAD for comparison
    local old_head
    old_head=$(git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    local output
    if output=$(git -C "$repo_path" pull "${pull_args[@]}" 2>&1); then
        local duration=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
        local new_head
        new_head=$(git -C "$repo_path" rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "")

        if [[ "$old_head" == "$new_head" ]]; then
            log_info "Already current: $repo_name"
            write_result "$repo_name" "pull" "current" "$duration" ""
        else
            log_success "Pulled: $repo_name (${duration}s)"
            write_result "$repo_name" "pull" "updated" "$duration" ""
        fi
        return 0
    else
        local exit_code=$?
        local reason="failed"

        if [[ "$output" =~ (divergent|cannot\ be\ fast-forwarded) ]]; then
            reason="diverged"
            log_warn "Diverged: $repo_name (needs manual merge or --rebase)"
        elif [[ "$output" =~ (conflict|CONFLICT) ]]; then
            reason="conflict"
            log_error "Merge conflict: $repo_name"
        else
            log_error "Pull failed: $repo_name"
        fi

        write_result "$repo_name" "pull" "$reason" "" "$output"
        return $exit_code
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All strategies work
- Detects current vs updated (by comparing HEADs, not strings)
- Categorizes failures correctly
- Records results","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.455221388-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.455221388-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all git operations: is_git_repo, get_repo_status, get_remote_url, check_remote_mismatch, do_clone, do_pull, do_fetch. All tests pass.","labels":["git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-807","depends_on_id":"bd-802","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.094464008-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-807","depends_on_id":"bd-405","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.126330962-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-808","title":"Create scripts/test_local_git.sh","description":"**Integration tests with local git repos**

## What
Test script that creates local git repos and tests operations against them.

## Why
We can't rely on network for CI tests. Local repos let us test all scenarios deterministically.

## Test Scenarios
```bash
# Setup
TEMP_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
trap "rm -rf $TEMP_DIR" EXIT

# Create 'remote' bare repo
git init --bare "$TEMP_DIR/remote.git"

# Clone and make commits
git clone "$TEMP_DIR/remote.git" "$TEMP_DIR/local"
cd "$TEMP_DIR/local"
echo "initial" > file.txt
git add . && git commit -m "Initial"
git push origin main

# Create 'projects dir' clone
git clone "$TEMP_DIR/remote.git" "$TEMP_DIR/projects/testrepo"

# Make new commit in 'remote' (via local)
echo "update" >> "$TEMP_DIR/local/file.txt"
git -C "$TEMP_DIR/local" add . && git -C "$TEMP_DIR/local" commit -m "Update"
git -C "$TEMP_DIR/local" push

# Test: status should show 'behind'
# Test: pull should update
# Test: dirty detection
# Test: diverged detection
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests run without network
- All status conditions tested
- CI can run this script","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["git","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-808","depends_on_id":"bd-802","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.156263651-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-808","depends_on_id":"bd-806","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.186539707-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-808","depends_on_id":"bd-807","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:11.216416421-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-809","title":"Handle branch not found gracefully","description":"**Graceful handling when repo@branch branch doesn't exist**

## What
When user specifies repo@branch and branch doesn't exist, handle gracefully.

## Why
Branches get deleted, renamed, or typos happen. Current plan doesn't address this.

## Behavior
```
$ ru sync owner/repo@nonexistent
Cloning: owner/repo
  Warning: Branch 'nonexistent' not found
  Falling back to default branch (main)
```

## Implementation
```bash
do_clone() {
    # ... clone repo ...
    
    if [[ -n "$branch" ]]; then
        if git -C "$target_dir" rev-parse --verify "origin/$branch" &>/dev/null; then
            git -C "$target_dir" checkout "$branch" --quiet
        else
            log_warn "Branch '$branch' not found, using default branch"
            write_result "$repo_name" "clone" "branch_not_found" "" "Branch: $branch"
        fi
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clone succeeds with warning
- Default branch used as fallback
- Result recorded for summary
- User informed clearly","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["error-handling","git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-809","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.797824099-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-809","depends_on_id":"bd-902","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.828864235-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-810","title":"Handle network timeouts gracefully","description":"**Handle network timeouts gracefully**

## What
Add timeout handling for clone and pull operations.

## Why
Flaky networks, slow connections, or overloaded servers can cause operations to hang indefinitely. Users need a way to bound wait time.

## Implementation
- Set GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_LIMIT=1000 and GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_TIME=30 as defaults
- These cause git to abort if transfer rate drops below 1KB/s for 30 seconds
- Add --timeout N option to override (sets GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_TIME)
- On timeout, log error and continue to next repo
- Report timeouts distinctly in summary (not just 'failed')

## Acceptance Criteria
- Slow operations time out after reasonable period
- Timeout errors are clearly reported
- Other repos continue processing after timeout
- Timeout can be configured via flag or config","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["resilience"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-810","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.921445046-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-810","depends_on_id":"bd-806","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.952318959-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-810","depends_on_id":"bd-807","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.981841826-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-9","title":"Phase 9: Repo List Management","description":"**EPIC: Repository List Loading & Management**

## Goal
Implement robust loading, parsing, and deduplication of repository lists from config files.

## Rationale
Users maintain lists of repos to sync. These lists support comments, blank lines, branch pinning (repo@branch), and custom local names (repo as custom-name). We must parse all this robustly and handle edge cases.

## List File Format
```
# Comments start with #
# Blank lines are ignored

https://github.com/owner/repo
owner/repo
owner/repo@develop
owner/repo as custom-name
```

## Deduplication
Multiple list files may reference the same repo. We dedupe by resolved local path, keeping the first occurrence. This prevents double-processing.

## Future: Tags
We're reserving syntax for tags: `owner/repo #tag=backend`. Not implementing now, but the parser should not break on this.

## Success Criteria
- List files parse correctly with all syntax variants
- Comments and blank lines are skipped
- Duplicates are detected and deduplicated
- Collisions are warned about","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.268499861-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:33:03.268499861-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all repo list management functions: parse_repo_spec (namerefs), load_repo_list, dedupe_repos, detect_collisions, get_all_repos. All tests pass.","labels":["lists","repos"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9","depends_on_id":"bd-3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.218431105-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-9","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.246730918-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
